articles.title: New York leads four-state primary elections, in photos
articles.summary:
New York’s closely watched primary elections took center stage Tuesday as congressional races tested the political influence of New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani, while voters also cast ballots in Maryland, South Carolina and Utah.

___

This is a photo gallery curated by AP photo editors.

articles.title: In New York primaries, progressives make inroads on establishment, and a Kennedy scion seeks office
articles.summary: Incumbent Democrats faced fierce challenges in New York's congressional primaries

articles.title: Warehouse fire spews potentially toxic smoke in California
articles.summary: A warehouse fire in Southern California has been spewing potentially toxic smoke for nearly a week near the Port of Los Angeles. Matt Gutman reports.

articles.title: Former Colorado analyst pleads guilty in DNA testing scandal
articles.summary: A former analyst with the Colorado Bureau of Investigation who was accused of cutting corners and bucking testing protocols has pleaded guilty to four felonies stemming from a DNA testing scandal

articles.title: COVID-19 vaccine study that was blocked from CDC journal is published elsewhere
articles.summary: A study on COVID-19 vaccine effectiveness has been published after being blocked from a government health journal

articles.title: Boaters compete in 750-mile race... with no engines
articles.summary: For the "Race to Alaska," intrepid boaters compete in a 750-mile no-engine boat race from Washington state to Alaska.

articles.title: Chinese supercomputer displaces US machines as world's fastest
articles.summary: A supercomputer in China now outranks its U.S. counterparts as the world’s most powerful, marking the first time since 2017 that a Chinese computer has topped a list sometimes viewed as a measure of a nation’s technological prowess

articles.title: 17 states and trade group sue California over plastics packaging law
articles.summary: A coalition of 17 states and a trade association are suing California to block a strict recycling law aimed at reducing plastic waste

articles.title: Fraught state fair opens on the National Mall
articles.summary: The Great American State Fair begins in D.C. as part of the country's 250th anniversary celebrations. It's bringing hundreds of exhibits to the National Mall, though some state aren't participating.

articles.title: GOP senators to meet Trump face-to-face at a time of growing frustration
articles.summary: Republican senators say they hope that a closed-door meeting with President Donald Trump is about unity, not disagreement

articles.title: California intends to sue Trump administration over deal to end offshore wind project
articles.summary: California intends to sue the Trump administration over its deal to end an offshore wind project proposed off the state’s central coast

articles.title: Army sergeant gets life sentence for shootings that wounded 5 at Georgia base
articles.summary: An Army sergeant has been sentenced to life in prison for shootings that wounded five people at a Georgia base last summer

articles.title: Alleged antifa members in Texas are sentenced for ICE protest
articles.summary: Eight people got prison terms ranging from 30 to 100 years for their roles in a protest that turned violent outside an immigration detention center last summer.

articles.title: UN adopts resolution to ensure perpetrators of crimes against peacekeepers face justice
articles.summary: The U.N. Security Council has unanimously adopted a resolution authorizing new steps to ensure that perpetrators of crimes against peacekeepers face justice

articles.title: Trump says 6 people have been arrested for damaging the Reflecting Pool
articles.summary: President Donald Trump says six people have been arrested for damaging the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool, which Trump recently ordered to be painted what he calls “American Flag Blue.”

articles.title: 8 convicted of terrorism charges in Texas immigration center shooting sentenced to decades in prison
articles.summary: A demonstrator who shot and wounded a police officer during a protest outside a Texas immigration center July 4 has been sentenced to 100 years in federal prison

articles.title: A shooter kills 2 at a Northern California library and an 18-year-old suspect is arrested
articles.summary: Police investigating a shooting at a library in Northern California that left two people dead say an 18-year-old suspect has been arrested

articles.title: N.Y. House primaries test Mamdani's influence, and more races to watch today
articles.summary: Voters are going to the polls Tuesday for contests in New York, South Carolina, Maryland and Utah.

articles.title: Surfside condo started failing 3 weeks before total collapse, federal report says
articles.summary: A new federal report shows the likely cause of the 2021 Surfside condo collapse in Florida that killed 98 people. According to the report, the structure failures began three weeks before the building fell when two connections between garage columns and the pool deck gave way.

articles.title: Trump again alleges vandals damaged Reflecting Pool: "They went in there with a knife"
articles.summary: President Trump is again blaming vandals for the issues with D.C.'s Reflecting Pool, but when asked, the president has still not offered evidence. The landmark is set to be drained for repairs. Ed O'Keefe reports.

articles.title: Trump says proof that vandals cut Reflecting Pool will be provided in court
articles.summary: President Trump has insisted that vandals, rather than questionable craftsmanship, are responsible for the enduring problems following the Reflecting Pool's $14.7 million sealant job.

articles.title: Feds fight planned sale of personal items salvaged from the Titanic
articles.summary: Court documents referenced RMS Titanic's plan to sell artifacts including a bronze cherub, a necklace of gold nuggets and a heart-shaped pendant.

articles.title: Former Oklahoma death row inmate back in court as case proceeds to retrial in 1997 murder case
articles.summary: A former Oklahoma death row inmate who was released from incarceration after nearly three decades is scheduled to be back in court as his case proceeds to a retrial for a 1997 killing that put him on the brink of execution three times

articles.title: Actor James Handy's alleged killer found mentally incompetent for prosecution
articles.summary: A judge has found that a man charged with murder in the stabbing of actor James Handy isn't mentally competent for criminal court proceedings.

articles.title: Millions of bees escape after truck crashes in Texas: "Remain indoors"
articles.summary: The owner of Moore Honey estimated that only about a quarter of the 408 hives would survive.

articles.title: How Trump's 'Complete and Total Endorsement' has reshaped the Republican Party
articles.summary: An NPR analysis of more than a thousand Trump endorsements in House, Senate and governor races over the last decade finds the president now picks candidates earlier — and in safer races.

articles.title: Severe weather tears through the Midwest, leaving at least 3 people dead
articles.summary: Thunderstorms and tornadoes have hit several communities over the past few days. The Midwest has seen a significant increase in the number of tornadoes.

articles.title: Pentagon seeks $80 billion from Congress for Iran war
articles.summary: The Pentagon has told lawmakers it needs roughly $80 billion, mostly to cover the cost of the war in Iran

articles.title: Coast Guard helicopter crashes on a training mission in Alaska and four crew members are injured
articles.summary: A Coast Guard helicopter has crashed in southeast Alaska

articles.title: Firefighters deliver conflicting testimony over fireworks in Palisades Fire arson trial
articles.summary: Los Angeles firefighters delivered conflicting testimony in the federal arson trial of Jonathan Rinderknecht, who is accused of starting the deadly Palisades Fire in Los Angeles

articles.title: What to know about a cold storage warehouse fire in Los Angeles
articles.summary: Firefighters are still battling a blaze at a massive frozen-food storage facility near downtown Los Angeles six days after the fire started.

articles.title: Authorities arrest 2 more suspects in planned attack on Trump's UFC show
articles.summary: Two more people in Missouri and Washington state have been arrested in connection with the planned attack. Law enforcement officials learned about the possible threat on June 10, four days before the event on the White House's South Lawn.

articles.title: South Carolina voters will choose between two Trump-backed governor candidates in Republican runoff
articles.summary: South Carolina voters are returning to the polls to finalize the Republican nominee for governor in a runoff election

articles.title: Redrawn US House map in Utah sets up heated Democratic primary in Salt Lake-area district
articles.summary: Utah Democrats are set to face off in the primary election for a redrawn U.S. House district that could help determine whether the party takes control of the narrowly divided chamber this fall

articles.title: A deadly collapse at a Florida condo happened slowly over several weeks, probe finds
articles.summary: Federal investigators have found that the deadly collapse of a Florida beachfront condominium actually started weeks before it left a massive pile of rubble

articles.title: Lionel Messi breaks record for most career World Cup goals
articles.summary: Lionel Messi made history Monday as he scored the 17th and 18th goals of his World Cup career, a new record. Lilia Luciano reports.

articles.title: Clive Davis, legendary music executive, dies at age 94
articles.summary: Clive Davis helped shape the careers of music stars including Janis Joplin, Bruce Springsteen and Whitney Houston.

articles.title: Trump's Forest Service says it's fully staffed as fires erupt in the West
articles.summary: The Forest Service says it's fully staffed with seasonal firefighters going into summer but there are still questions about whether the government is prepared if major wildfires get out of hand.

articles.title: Prosecutors allege mob hitman turned councilman in New Jersey has returned to crime
articles.summary: A notorious mob hitman who once testified against John “Junior” Gotti before cleaning up his life enough to become a councilman in New Jersey has been arrested on charges that, if proven, reflect a return to the lifestyle of his youth

articles.title: A federal judge finds a Trump data system to verify voters is unlawful
articles.summary: Tens of millions of voters have had their data run through the Trump administration's revamped SAVE tool. A judge just found it unlawful.

articles.title: Company owned by Trump donor won $1.7 million no-bid Reflecting Pool contract
articles.summary: The federal government awarded a company owned by a Trump donor $1.7 million to install a new water cleaning system for the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool, records show.

articles.title: A plan to sell artifacts from the Titanic faces US government opposition
articles.summary: A newly unsealed court filing shows the U.S. government opposes a plan to auction more than 100 artifacts recovered from the wreckage of the Titanic in the North Atlantic

articles.title: Millions of honeybees escape into a Texas neighborhood after a semitrailer crash
articles.summary: Officials say millions of honeybees escaped into a rural Texas neighborhood after a semitrailer carrying about 400 hives tipped over

articles.title: Quarantine comes to an end for the last of the hantavirus ship passengers in Nebraska
articles.summary: The last eight Americans who endured 42 days in a specialized hospital quarantine unit after exposure to an unusual hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship that killed three people have left the Nebraska facility

articles.title: Judge blocks Trump administration's database of Americans' personal info
articles.summary: U.S. District Judge Sparkle Sooknanan said the administration violated the law when it created a centralized database of Americans' personal records.

articles.title: Family of 1-year-old killed by police at a Mississippi Walmart wants video released
articles.summary: The family of a 1-year-old boy killed by police in Mississippi is calling on authorities to release video of the shooting

articles.title: Supreme Court reinstates murder conviction in case of Etan Patz, missing NYC boy
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has reinstated a murder conviction in the 1979 disappearance of 6-year-old Etan Patz

articles.title: Michigan floods expose lack of information, preparation in many rural areas
articles.summary: Many homeowners hit by devastating Michigan floods had no insurance and no idea they were at risk — underscoring vulnerabilities throughout rural America as climate change causes more extreme weather

articles.title: Asian shares are mixed and US futures fall as Iran talks make progress
articles.summary: Trading in U.S. markets is mixed while oil prices are edging lower on fresh optimism over progress in U.S.-Iran negotiations

articles.title: Los Angeles schools superintendent resigns after FBI search and months on paid leave
articles.summary: The superintendent of Los Angeles public schools has resigned four months after he was put on paid leave during a federal investigation

articles.title: New York's congressional candidates make final case in last day before primary
articles.summary: New York’s congressional candidates will deliver closing arguments ahead of primary elections Tuesday, as an ascendent progressive left takes on establishment Democrats

articles.title: Judge in Charlie Kirk killing case to decide if prosecutors could be punished for comments in media
articles.summary: The Utah judge in the murder case over Charlie Kirk’s killing says he will rule Monday whether prosecutors could face sanctions for comments to the media about a bullet fragment recovered from the conservative activist’s body

articles.title: China hits back at US sanctions on tech giants, restricting its exports to American defense firms
articles.summary: China has announced sanctions on 10 American defense companies

articles.title: What a reporter found when uncovering why federal agents allowed a deadly drug to hit the streets
articles.summary: Associated Press journalist Jim Mustian explains how he reported and wrote a story that examined why the U.S. Drug Enforcement Administration allowed hundreds of thousands of fentanyl pills to hit the streets of New Mexico from 2023 to 2025

articles.title: Iran's challenges at World Cup swirl outside draw with Belgium
articles.summary: Iran took another important step toward advancing in the World Cup, but once again the off-the-field play dominated the conversation

articles.title: US strike on an alleged drug boat kills 2, leaves 6 survivors, in the Pacific Ocean
articles.summary: The U.S. military has conduced another strike against a boat accused of smuggling drugs in the eastern Pacific Ocean, immediately killing two people and leaving six survivors amid an ongoing campaign against alleged traffickers in Latin America

articles.title: Wyndham Clark wins his second U.S. Open title with wire-to-wire victory at Shinnecock Hills
articles.summary: Wyndham Clark wins his second U.S. Open title with wire-to-wire victory at Shinnecock Hills

articles.title: LA Mayor Bass declares emergency to secure resources to help fight warehouse fire
articles.summary: Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass declared an emergency Saturday to ensure the city gets the resources it needs to fight a large warehouse fire that has sent large plumes of smoke into the air

articles.title: Rips appear in Reflecting Pool's new sealant after algae bloom turn water green
articles.summary: President Trump's efforts to spruce up the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool faced a snag this week, with algae turning the water green while rips appeared in an "American Flag Blue" surface picked by the president.

articles.title: Trump says he'll resurface Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool, turning it blue
articles.summary: President Trump's renovation kick has now reached the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool.
